title: Lepidaploa chiriquiensis
taxonomy: Lepidaploa chiriquiensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Lepidaploa, which is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas. Lepidaploa chiriquiensis is further classified within the tribe Liabeae, a group of plants known for their distinctive flower structures. The plant's classification is also influenced by its relationships to other species within the Asteraceae family. Lepidaploa chiriquiensis is a specific and unique species within this broader classification.

The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can help in treating various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
